headline: TouchAI
subhead: A universal touch language, runtime, and adapters so AI can read intention from physical interaction—and answer with structured haptics—not only text and voice.

layers.title: Input
layers.subtitle: Touch Language
layers.body: Normalized touch streams and gesture tokens: the user speaks in touch; devices emit a versioned wire format the model can learn.

layers.title: Output
layers.subtitle: Haptics
layers.body: Semantic haptics (ack, confirm, clarify…) compile to neutral pulse programs, then map to Core Haptics, Android Vibrator, or the web.

layers.title: Infrastructure
layers.subtitle: Programs & envelopes
layers.body: Declarative rules and JSONL envelopes tie sessions, intents, and haptics together for reproducible datasets and cross-device runtimes.

packages.name: @touchai/touch-spec
packages.summary: Zod + TS wire types: TouchSample, GestureToken, HapticProgram, TouchEventEnvelope, TouchProgram.

packages.name: @touchai/touch-runtime
packages.summary: Gesture segmentation, rule evaluation, semantic haptic materialization.

packages.name: @touchai/touch-dataset
packages.summary: JSONL append/parse for envelopes — training and telemetry pipelines.

packages.name: @touchai/touch-adapter-web
packages.summary: PointerEvent → TouchSample and HapticProgram → Vibration API.

packages.name: touch-adapter-android
packages.summary: Kotlin: MotionEvent → TouchSample, pulse playback via VibratorEffect.

packages.name: public/touchai/*
packages.summary: Build-free browser mirror of the runtime so this page runs Touch Language end-to-end.

next: Personalization layer: per-session normalization of velocity/pressure before segmentation
Multi-pointer gesture tokens (two-finger swipe, pinch) in the spec and runtime
DSL frontend that compiles a textual program (.touch) to TouchProgram JSON
Cloud ingest endpoint that only accepts TouchEventEnvelope with the matching spec version
